milestone: A2 (A-b): crash-safe in-place COW commits (interrupted-commit rollback)
status: Passed (macOS kernel rolls a crash-interrupted commit back to the previous checkpoint intact; fsck_apfs fully clean after the IP free-queue landed - the previous cosmetic internal-pool note is retired). Crash-safety certified.
capability: Two rotations make an interrupted in-place commit roll back cleanly. (1) The chunk-info block + allocation bitmap copy-on-write into the spare internal-pool slot (nextIpSlot round-robin 187->189->185->187), the spaceman cib_addr moves to the new cib in the re-emitted spaceman, and the cib's o_oid moves with it (a physical object's o_oid must equal its block). (2) The COW chain allocates from one of four disjoint regions chosen by xid ((newXid-3)%4; region 0 = the genesis free region so single commits are byte-identical), so a commit never lands on the blocks the previous few checkpoints still reference (descriptor ring depth 4). The live cib/bitmap are resolved from the spaceman before allocation (loadLiveAllocationSlot), since after the first rotation they are no longer the fixed genesis 187/188.
host_verify: Unit test apfsWriter_crashBeforeCheckpointDurableRollsBack: 3 chained commits (alpha, bravo, charlie); the third's descriptor-ring checkpoint is voided (desc_base+desc_index, 2 blocks) and block 0 reverted to the previous checkpoint to simulate a crash before durability; the reader lists the previous checkpoint's two files (charlie discarded). Block scan confirms disjoint regions (xid3 201-206, xid4 265-270, xid5 329-334) so the previous commit's blocks survive. 124/124 unit tests pass.

vm_external_gate.environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery; apfs.kext 2332.101.1; the crash-simulated container (volume ROLLBACK) attached raw as SATA disk0
method: 1. format ROLLBACK 64 MiB -> commit alpha -> commit bravo (last committed) -> commit charlie (interrupted).
2. Crash before durability: revert block 0 to the bravo checkpoint and zero charlie's descriptor-ring checkpoint.
3. Apple kernel auto-mounted /Volumes/ROLLBACK; `ls` showed alpha.txt + bravo.txt with charlie.txt absent (rolled back).
4. diskutil unmount; fsck_apfs -n /dev/rdisk0 -> Checking the checkpoint with transaction ID 4 (rolled back, not 5); The container appears to be OK.
vm_external_gate.kernel_behavior: macOS kernel rolled the interrupted commit back to the previous checkpoint and mounted it read-write (ls = alpha.txt, bravo.txt; charlie.txt discarded).
vm_external_gate.apple_fsck_apfs: Checking the checkpoint with transaction ID 4; volume ROLLBACK formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer; fsroot tree / extent ref tree / free queue trees / volume object map space checked; The container /dev/rdisk0 appears to be OK.
vm_external_gate.evidence_png: apfs-a2-crashsafe-rollback-fsck-PASS.png (rollback) and apfs-a2-crashsafe-rotation-fsck-PASS.png (rotated container at rest)

residual: RESOLVED. The cosmetic fsck_apfs note 'overallocation detected in internal pool (0xbb+2)' has been retired by implementing the IP free-queue (commit 089f7ff). Root cause: the crash-safe commit rotated the cib/bitmap through the spare slot but never recorded the freed slot, so once the macOS kernel continued the container its stricter IP free-count cross-check flagged the genesis cib slot 187,188 as overallocated. Harvested the kernel continuing a generated container (a2base = genesis + five kernel commits): on every cib rotation it keeps a rolling two-deep window in the IP free-queue tree - the slot freed the previous commit plus the slot freed this commit, each two ghost blocks {xid,paddr,len=2} sorted by xid - and sets sm_fq[IP].sfq_count to the pending total and sfq_oldest_xid to the window's oldest xid. Both slots are computable from the rotation (rotation.liveCib freed now; rotation.freeCib = nextIpSlot(newCib) is the previous freed slot, == the genesis seed at the first commit), so the writer rebuilds the IP free-queue tree (buildFreeQueueLeaf) + spaceman counts with no tree read. The generated xid3 container is now byte-for-byte the kernel's xid3 free-queue state (fq tree nkeys=2 [{2,185,2},{3,187,2}], sfq_count=4, oldest_xid=2). Apple-verified: the macOS Sequoia kernel auto-mounted it read-write and fsck_apfs -n is now FULLY clean - 'Checking the space manager free queue trees' passes with no internal-pool note and 'The container appears to be OK' with zero warnings (evidence apfs-a2-ipfreequeue-fsck-PASS.png). The earlier 642ed99 (sm_ip_bitmap ring) and dd87c2d (sfq_oldest_xid) were harvest-faithful steps toward this; the ring advance is structurally correct, the oldest_xid is now derived from the free-queue window. The four-region chain allocator still caps a single commit's chain+data at the region size; the same free-queue is the general solution for arbitrary file sizes per commit.
gates: clang-format + lizard + cppcheck clean; commits 1240750 (cib/bitmap rotation), 70aa6d8 (COW chain region rotation + rollback test), fd18349 (cib o_oid), 642ed99 (sm_ip_bitmap ring advance), dd87c2d (sfq_oldest_xid), 089f7ff (IP free-queue, retires the internal-pool note) - all pre-commit hooks passed, no bypass.
next: Free-queue for the main device (arbitrary file sizes per commit, generalizing the IP free-queue), multi-CIB (>256 MiB), then the A-b matrix flip (physical-USB destructive + this crash/rollback on Apple-written media).
